title: medical_emergency
description: A medical emergency occurs during a busy shift.

timeline.title: Initial Collapse
timeline.description: Staff member collapses in the cafeteria. Check for responsiveness and call for help.

timeline.title: First Aid Response
timeline.description: Trained responders arrive on scene. Begin CPR and retrieve AED. Call 911 immediately.

timeline.title: EMS Notification
timeline.description: Emergency Medical Services dispatched, ETA 6 minutes. Continue first aid and monitor vitals.

timeline.title: Patient Stabilization
timeline.description: Patient shows signs of improvement. Maintain care and prepare for EMS handoff.

timeline.title: Transport Preparation
timeline.description: EMS arrived. Clear access routes and provide patient information to paramedics.

timeline.title: Site Cleanup and Debriefing
timeline.description: Patient transported to hospital. Secure AED, document incident, and offer support to witnesses.

node-1.title: Staff Member Collapses
node-1.situation: A staff member has collapsed suddenly in the cafeteria during lunch. Bystanders are unsure whether the person is conscious.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Check for responsiveness, call for help loudly, and send someone to call 911 and retrieve the AED.

options.label: B
options.text: Give the person space and wait a few minutes to see if they recover on their own.

options.label: C
options.text: Move the person to a more comfortable position before calling for help.

node-2.title: Beginning First Aid
node-2.situation: The patient is unresponsive and not breathing normally. A trained first aider is on scene along with an AED. EMS is en route.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Begin CPR immediately and prepare the AED for use as directed by the device.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for EMS to arrive before starting any treatment to avoid causing harm.

node-3.title: Patient Showing Signs of Response
node-3.situation: After CPR and AED use, the patient has regained a pulse. EMS will arrive in approximately 2 minutes.
node-3.question: What do you do while waiting for EMS?

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain monitoring, keep the patient still, and clear the area so EMS can access the scene.

options.label: B
options.text: Help the patient to a chair so they are more comfortable.

node-4.title: EMS On Scene
node-4.situation: Paramedics have arrived and are taking over care of the patient. They need patient information and access to transport the patient.
node-4.question: How do you assist the EMS team?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide patient details, clear a direct route to the exit, and prepare to document the incident.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow observers to remain nearby so they can provide moral support to the patient.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The patient has been transported to hospital. The incident has been documented, the AED is secured, and support has been offered to witnesses.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
